data.narrative:
Hadean is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms—all operating in the Defense & Security sector at similar stages. The primary private comparables include Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two methodologies triangulate Hadean's value. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $40M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $76M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same-stage private companies with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B, yielding a median of $1.2B. The wide spread between these methods reflects material differences in how capital efficiency and peer comparability anchor valuation in this sector.

The triangulated range spans $65M (low), $638M (median), to $1.4B (high). The peer-median method dominates the upper bound, while stage-progression alone produces a significantly lower estimate. Key uncertainties include Hadean's revenue traction and growth rate relative to named peers—critical inputs absent from available data—and whether the defense-tech peer set fairly reflects Hadean's specific market position and contract runway. Reconciling these methods requires clarity on unit economics and customer concentration.
